title: SOC 2 CC6.7 — System boundary & data-transmission control: unapproved egress destinations
exclusions: Does NOT block traffic; detects outbound flows whose destination ASN/country is absent from the approved-egress registry for the originating service.
evidence: Saved search 'soc2_cc6_7_egress_boundary' (every 15m) outputs src_service, dest_ip, dest_asn, dest_country, approved, to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: CC6.7 Information transmission and disposal — unapproved egress is the direct failure.

controlTest.positiveScenario: An outbound flow from the payments service to an ASN not in approved_egress.csv is surfaced within 15m.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A flow to an approved ASN+country tuple is absent.

dataSources: `index=netfw` sourcetype=pan:traffic; ASN/country enrichment via `asn_country.csv`; approved-egress registry `approved_egress.csv`.
app: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757)
spl:
index=netfw sourcetype=pan:traffic action=allow direction=outbound earliest=-15m
| rename src AS src_ip dest AS dest_ip
| lookup service_by_src.csv src_ip OUTPUT src_service
| lookup asn_country.csv ip=dest_ip OUTPUT asn AS dest_asn country AS dest_country
| lookup approved_egress.csv src_service dest_asn OUTPUT approved
| where isnull(approved) OR approved!="yes"
| stats count sum(bytes_out) AS bytes BY src_service dest_ip dest_asn dest_country
dataModelAcceleration: Enable acceleration for the Authentication data model (Authentication.Authentication).
description: Evaluates outbound flows against the per-service approved-egress registry. A flow to an unapproved ASN or country is a direct CC6.7 boundary violation.
value: Provides the evidence SOC 2 auditors want: proof that outbound flows are governed and deviations are captured. Supports reduction of shadow SaaS risk.
grandmaExplanation: We keep evidence for CC6.7 — System boundary & data-transmission control: unapproved egress destinations. SOC 2 is how we show we run our services responsibly, and we keep evidence that supports that story.
implementation: (1) Enrich Palo Alto traffic with service/ASN/country; (2) maintain approved_egress.csv; (3) schedule UC every 15m; (4) hit opens a ticket to service owner; (5) 48h SLA; (6) weekly review by Platform team. Indexes required: `index=netfw`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=pan:traffic`, `sourcetype=pan:traffic;`.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=netfw` sourcetype=pan:traffic; ASN/country enrichment via `asn_country.csv`; approved-egress registry `approved_egress.csv`..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Enrich Palo Alto traffic with service/ASN/country; (2) maintain approved_egress.csv; (3) schedule UC every 15m; (4) hit opens a ticket to service owner; (5) 48h SLA; (6) weekly review by Platform team.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=netfw sourcetype=pan:traffic action=allow direction=outbound earliest=-15m
| rename src AS src_ip dest AS dest_ip
| lookup service_by_src.csv src_ip OUTPUT src_service
| lookup asn_country.csv ip=dest_ip OUTPUT asn AS dest_asn country AS dest_country
| lookup approved_egress.csv src_service dest_asn OUTPUT approved
| where isnull(approved) OR approved!="yes"
| stats count sum(bytes_out) AS bytes BY src_service dest_ip dest_asn dest_country
```

Understanding this SPL

**SOC 2 CC6.7 — System boundary & data-transmission control: unapproved egress destinations** — Provides the evidence SOC 2 auditors want: proof that outbound flows are governed and deviations are captured. Supports reduction of shadow SaaS risk.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=netfw` sourcetype=pan:traffic; ASN/country enrichment via `asn_country.csv`; approved-egress registry `approved_egress.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: netfw; **sourcetype**: pan:traffic. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=netfw, sourcetype=pan:traffic, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Renames fields with `rename` for clarity or joins.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(approved) OR approved!="yes"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by src_service dest_ip dest_asn dest_country** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the GRC system, audit register, or framework crosswalk. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Geo map of unapproved destinations, bar chart by service, single value 'unapproved egress flows (1h)'.
visualization: Geo map of unapproved destinations, bar chart by service, single value 'unapproved egress flows (1h)'. Key fields: `bytes`, `dest_asn`, `dest_country`, `dest_ip`, `src_ip`, `src_service`.
knownFalsePositives: Control test failures during audit windows, immature control rollouts, or known compensating-control gaps.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src span=1d | sort - count
